title: cuscuta obtusiflora subsp. breviflora
intro: Cuscuta obtusiflora subsp. breviflora, also known as the Dodder plant, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cuscuta obtusiflora subsp. breviflora include improving fertility in both men and women, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting the immune system. Cuscuta obtusiflora subsp. breviflora can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
